LAW AND MOTION 302, (1) CROSS DEFENDANT BAY CITY MECHANICAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ ADJUDICATION. RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. HE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION BURDEN. EVEN IF THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE CERTIFICATE OF COMPLETION, BCM FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE WHEN THE STATUTE OF REPOSE COMMENCED. EVEN IF IT TRUE THAT THE CERTIFICATE OF COMPLETION WAS ISSUED ON MARCH 12, 2001 (KHADLIKAR DECLARATION, EX. E), CCP 337.15(G)(2) STATES THAT THE TRIGGER DATE RUNS FROM "THE DATE OF RECORDATION OF A VALID NOTICE OF COMPLETION." IN THIS CASE, THE COURT HAS NO EVIDENCE REGARDING THE DATE OF RECORDATION. BCM RELIES SOLELY ON THE CERTIFICATE OF COMPLETION AS THE TRIGGER DATE. SEE BCM'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AT 6:4-6; 12:4-8 & UMF 18, 22-31. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication;
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jessica Ryland, Esq., 415.281.7619, and Steven Cvitanovic, Esq., 415.281.7608, for Cross Complainant James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corporation.
Miloslav Khadilkar, Esq., 925.934.6102, and Truman Phan-Quang, Esq., 415.777.1308 x224, for Cross Defendant Bay City Mechanical, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT BAY CITY MECHANICAL, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication is denied. Bay City Mechanical ('BCM') has not met its initial burden. BCM entered into a Subcontract with James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corp. ('JERO'). (UMF no. 3.) The Subcontract states '[JERO] General Terms and Conditions attached are hereby made part of this agreement.' (Decl. of Bobby Amos, Ex. A.) The Terms and Conditions state in the first sentence that 'Copies of the General Contract'are in the office of the Contractor and have been examined by the Subcontractor 'Subcontractor agrees that he is familiar with all of the terms, conditions, and obligations of the general contract'and that he enters into this Agreement based upon his investigation of all such matters and is in no way relying upon any representations of Contractor'It is further agreed that the contract documents are incorporated in this Agreement by this reference with the same force and effect as if they were set forth herein, and that Subcontractor will be bound by any and all of said contract documents insofar as they relate in any way, to the work covered by the Agreement. Subcontractor agrees to be bound to the Contractor in the same manner and to the same extent as Contractor is bound to Owner under the contract documents.' (Decl. of James Orellana, Ex. 1.) The Prime Contract provides at Sec. 13.7 that 'Notwithstanding any of the foregoing to the contrary, the statutory limitations period with respect to 'latent deficiencies''shall commence upon the later of (1) the time described at Paragraph 13.7.1 above or (2) upon the Owner's discovery of the Latent Deficiency and its cause.' (Decl. of Miloslav Khadilkar, Ex. G.) CCP sec. 337.15 is 'an ordinary, procedural statute of limitations.' (Brisbane Lodging L.P. v. Webcor Builders, Inc. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 1249, 1258.) Sophisticated parties may contractually alter the statute of limitations. (Id. at 1263, '[W]here the parties are on equal footing and where there was a considerable sophisticated give and take over the terms of the contract, those parties should be given the ability to enjoy the freedom of contract and to structure risk shifting as they see fit without judicial intervention.') Thus, BCM and JERO agreed, at the time they entered into the subcontract, that BCM would be bound by the Prime Contract, including Sec. 13.7. BCM presents no evidence that it was not a sophisticated party at the time of contracting or that it did not have the opportunity to negotiate the subcontract. The unambiguous language of the subcontract states that BCM agreed that it was familiar with the terms of the Prime Contract at the time it entered into the subcontract. The evidence shows that the earliest the Owner could have become aware of defective construction was in 2004. (UMF no. 15.) The complaint in this action was filed on October 24, 2011, less than 10 years from the earliest date the Owner could have discovered any defects. (UMF no. 27.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR5976

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Miloslav Khadilkar, Esq. of Van De Poel, Levy & Allen, LLP, Tel. 925.934.6102, for defendant Bay City Mechanical;
Steven M. Cvitanovic, Esq., Tel. 415.281.7608 and Jessica M. Lassere, Esq., Tel. 415.281.7619, of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP, for defendant/cross complainant Roberts-Obayashi.

(1) CROSS DEFENDANT BAY CITY MECHANICAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ Adjudication. RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. he motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied without prejudice. Defendant fails to maintain its initial summary judgment/adjudication burden. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of the certificate of completion, BCM fails to demonstrate when the statute of repose commenced. Even if it true that the Certificate of Completion was issued on March 12, 2001 (Khadlikar Declaration, Ex. E), CCP 337.15(g)(2) states that the trigger date runs from "the date of recordation of a valid notice of completion." In this case, the Court has no evidence regarding the date of recordation. BCM relies solely on the Certificate of Completion as the trigger date. See BCM's Memorandum of Points and Authorities at 6:4-6; 12:4-8 & UMF 18, 22-31. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CCP 473c(g) and CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

(2) James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corp.'s Motion for Amendment to Cross-Complaint. RULING: The matter was not argued. The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corp.'s Motion for Amendment to Cross-Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. The trial date is continued to March 17, 2014. The discovery cutoff is based on the new trial date. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
